task_updates.executor_notes: Implemented Strategy A with a shared pure utility in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/render-bounds.ts`. `VisualClip.tsx` now renders manual/positioned media through an intersected viewport layout derived from `computeViewportMediaLayout()`, so the DOM element is clipped to the visible composition intersection while preserving the intended cover slice. `OverlayEditor.tsx` now applies the same display-only `computeRenderBounds()` intersection when positioning overlay boxes, ghosts, and crop masks. `getClipBounds()` was not modified, and `startFullBounds`, crop math, and `onOverlayChange()` still operate on raw clip bounds only. Verification: focused lint passed, the new render-bounds unit test passed, and `tsc --noEmit` passed.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. Render transforms stayed entirely out of `getClipBounds()`: that function and the downstream edit-state flow (`startFullBounds`, crop math, `onOverlayChange()`) were left untouched. The shared utility is `src/tools/video-editor/lib/render-bounds.ts`; `VisualClip.tsx` uses `computeViewportMediaLayout()` for render-time clipping, and `OverlayEditor.tsx` uses `computeRenderBounds()` only in the display-positioning path.
